title: Infoblox DHCP Scope Utilization Trending Toward Exhaustion (Infoblox)
dataSources: `sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp` DHCPACK/lease events; scope metadata from Grid or lookup `infoblox_scopes.csv` with columns scope_name, network_cidr, total_leases
spl:
index=dhcp sourcetype="infoblox:dhcp" earliest=-7d@d latest=now
| where match(_raw,"(?i)DHCPACK|lease.*granted")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)scope[\s:=]+(?<scope_id>[^,\s]+)"
| stats dc(mac) as active_leases by scope_id, host
| lookup infoblox_scopes.csv scope_id OUTPUT pool_size network
| eval util_pct=if(isnotnull(pool_size) AND pool_size>0, round(100*active_leases/pool_size,1), null())
| where util_pct>=85 OR active_leases>=500
| sort - util_pct
description: Scopes that quietly fill during BYOD spikes, IoT rollouts, or VLAN migrations cause outages that look like 'random Wi-Fi failures.' Trending utilization from DHCP traffic catches exhaustion before clients fail to obtain addresses.
value: Network operations can expand pools, shorten lease times, or segment traffic proactively instead of reacting to ticket storms.
implementation: Ensure DHCP service logs include scope/network identifiers. Build a daily summary of distinct active leases per scope; join scope size from a CMDB-exported lookup. Alert when trailing-seven-day max utilization exceeds 85% or week-over-week growth exceeds 20%.
visualization: Timechart (utilization % per scope), single-value (worst scope), table (scope, leases in use, free, trend).

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Sourcetypes for the Splunk Add-on for Infoblox

grandmaExplanation: We track how full your Infoblox DHCP address pools are getting over time so you can add space before people lose connectivity.
